title: About the compatibility change between podman 3.2.1 and docker
body:
I use podman + buildpack to build my application in one of my systems. Recently, I upgraded podman from v3.1.2 to 3.2.1, and the system failed; It does not pull the image when it does not exist.

* Start podman as docker style first `podman system service --time 0 --log-level debug`
* Execute the following command in another console:
```
(curl -sSL "https://github.com/buildpacks/pack/releases/download/v0.19.0/pack-v0.19.0-linux.tgz" | tar -C /usr/local/bin/ --no-same-owner -xzv pack)
git clone https://github.com/buildpacks/samples
export DOCKER_HOST=unix:///var/run/podman/podman.sock
pack build sample-app --path samples/apps/java-maven --builder docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic --docker-host unix:///var/run/podman/podman.sock
```


* There will be the following logs in podman service

```
DEBU[0081] Pulling image cnbs/sample-builder:bionic (policy: always) 
DEBU[0081] Looking up image "cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" in local containers storage 
DEBU[0081] Trying "cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" ...      
DEBU[0081] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf" 
DEBU[0081] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf" 
DEBU[0081] Trying "localhost/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" ... 
DEBU[0081] Trying "registry.fedoraproject.org/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" ... 
DEBU[0081] Trying "registry.access.redhat.com/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" ... 
DEBU[0081] Trying "docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" ... 
DEBU[0081] Trying "quay.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" ... 
DEBU[0081] Trying "docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" ... 
DEBU[0081] APIHandler(90d916ae-d5db-4fc6-832d-200f0bed4d3b) -- POST /v1.38/images/create?fromImage=cnbs%2Fsample-builder&tag=bionic END 
DEBU[0081] IdleTracker 0xc000296008:idle 1m+0h/1t connection(s) 
DEBU[0081] IdleTracker 0xc000296008:active 1m+0h/1t connection(s) 
INFO[0081] APIHandler(c398db82-e605-4a24-9af7-0e3073d1ad4d) -- GET /v1.38/info BEGIN 
DEBU[0081] APIHandler(c398db82-e605-4a24-9af7-0e3073d1ad4d) -- Header: User-Agent=[Go-http-client/1.1] 
DEBU[0081] APIHandler(c398db82-e605-4a24-9af7-0e3073d1ad4d) -- GET /v1.38/info END 
DEBU[0081] IdleTracker 0xc000296008:idle 1m+0h/1t connection(s) 
DEBU[0081] IdleTracker 0xc000296008:active 1m+0h/1t connection(s) 
INFO[0081] APIHandler(8455db88-8510-4ef9-8a53-a817bd6f6b99) -- GET /v1.38/images/index.docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic/json BEGIN 
DEBU[0081] APIHandler(8455db88-8510-4ef9-8a53-a817bd6f6b99) -- Header: User-Agent=[Go-http-client/1.1] 
DEBU[0081] Looking up image "index.docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" in local containers storage 
DEBU[0081] Trying "index.docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" ... 
DEBU[0081] Trying "docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" ... 
DEBU[0081] Trying "docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic" ... 
INFO[0081] Request Failed(Not Found): failed to find image index.docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic: index.docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic: No such image 
DEBU[0081] APIHandler(8455db88-8510-4ef9-8a53-a817bd6f6b99) -- GET /v1.38/images/index.docker.io/cnbs/sample-builder:bionic/json END 
DEBU[0081] IdleTracker 0xc000296008:idle 1m+0h/1t connection(s) 
DEBU[0081] IdleTracker 0xc000296008:closed 1m+0h/1t connection(s) 
```


But if the podman version is 3.1.2; He will start to pull the nonexistent image

comments.body: @duanhongyi, did you resolve your issue?  Can you share how?

@vrothberg

It seems that the default configuration file of `quay.io/podman/stable:v3.2.1` has changed. I have no problem using the configuration file of v3.1.2
